Lines.FullText: Contains standard information about an openDAQ device and device type. The Device Info object is a

Elements.RawText: Contains standard information about an openDAQ device and device type. The Device Info object is a

Lines.FullText: Property Object, allowing for custom String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties to be added.

Elements.RawText: Property Object, allowing for custom String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties to be added.

Brief.RawText:
* @brief Contains standard information about an openDAQ device and device type. The Device Info object is a
 * Property Object, allowing for custom String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties to be added.

Lines.FullText: As the Device Info object adheres to the OPC UA: Devices standard, it behaves differently than

Elements.RawText: As the Device Info object adheres to the OPC UA: Devices standard, it behaves differently than

Lines.FullText: standard Property Objects. No metadata except the Value Type and Default Value are published

Elements.RawText: standard Property Objects. No metadata except the Value Type and Default Value are published

Lines.FullText: via OPC UA, and this only said Property metadata is visible to any clients.

Elements.RawText: via OPC UA, and this only said Property metadata is visible to any clients.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * As the Device Info object adheres to the OPC UA: Devices standard, it behaves differently than
 * standard Property Objects. No metadata except the Value Type and Default Value are published
 * via OPC UA, and this only said Property metadata is visible to any clients.

Lines.FullText: All fields - default (e.g. platform, manufacturer...) and custom are represented as either:

Elements.RawText: All fields - default (e.g. platform, manufacturer...) and custom are represented as either:

Tags.RawText:
*
 * All fields - default (e.g. platform, manufacturer...) and custom are represented as either:

Lines.FullText: - String-type properties

Elements.RawText: - String-type properties

Lines.FullText: - Integer-type properties

Elements.RawText: - Integer-type properties

Lines.FullText: - Bool-type properties

Elements.RawText: - Bool-type properties

Lines.FullText: - Float type properties

Elements.RawText: - Float type properties

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - String-type properties
 * - Integer-type properties
 * - Bool-type properties
 * - Float type properties

Lines.FullText: As such, listing all properties via Property Object methods, will return both the

Elements.RawText: As such, listing all properties via Property Object methods, will return both the

Lines.FullText: names of the default and custom properties. All default properties are initialized to an empty

Elements.RawText: names of the default and custom properties. All default properties are initialized to an empty

Lines.FullText: string except for RevisionCounter and Position that are integer properties and are

Elements.RawText: string except for RevisionCounter and Position that are integer properties and are

Lines.FullText: thus initialized to '0'. The names of the properties are written in camelCase - for

Elements.RawText: thus initialized to '0'. The names of the properties are written in camelCase - for

Lines.FullText: example "systemUuid", "parentMacAddress", "manufacturerUri".

Elements.RawText: example "systemUuid", "parentMacAddress", "manufacturerUri".

Tags.RawText:
*
 * As such, listing all properties via Property Object methods, will return both the
 * names of the default and custom properties. All default properties are initialized to an empty
 * string except for RevisionCounter and Position that are integer properties and are
 * thus initialized to '0'. The names of the properties are written in camelCase - for
 * example "systemUuid", "parentMacAddress", "manufacturerUri".

Lines.FullText: If the DeviceInfo object is obtained from a device, or when listing available devices, the

Elements.RawText: If the DeviceInfo object is obtained from a device, or when listing available devices, the

Lines.FullText: object is frozen (immutable). As such, Property Object setter methods cannot be used

Elements.RawText: object is frozen (immutable). As such, Property Object setter methods cannot be used

Lines.FullText: and will fail when called.

Elements.RawText: and will fail when called.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * If the DeviceInfo object is obtained from a device, or when listing available devices, the
 * object is frozen (immutable). As such, Property Object setter methods cannot be used
 * and will fail when called.

Lines.FullText: The getter methods represent a standardized set of Device properties according to the

Elements.RawText: The getter methods represent a standardized set of Device properties according to the

Lines.FullText: OPC UA: Devices standard. Any additional String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties can be added, using the

Elements.RawText: OPC UA: Devices standard. Any additional String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties can be added, using the

Lines.FullText: appropriate Property Object "add property" method. Any other types of properties are invalid.

Elements.RawText: appropriate Property Object "add property" method. Any other types of properties are invalid.

Lines.FullText: Although Integer-type properties are valid additions, Selection properties cannot be added to

Elements.RawText: Although Integer-type properties are valid additions, Selection properties cannot be added to

Lines.FullText: Device Info.

Elements.RawText: Device Info.

Description.RawText:
*
 * The getter methods represent a standardized set of Device properties according to the
 * OPC UA: Devices standard. Any additional String, Int, Bool, or Float-type properties can be added, using the
 * appropriate Property Object "add property" method. Any other types of properties are invalid.
 * Although Integer-type properties are valid additions, Selection properties cannot be added to
 * Device Info.
